channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Ireland's Controversial Try in Rugby Match Sparks Debate
item.description: In a closely contested rugby union match, Ireland's Jamison Gibson-Park scored a try that sparked significant debate among fans and officials. The try, which occurred on the stroke of half-time, was a...

item.title: Norovirus Outbreak Affects Over 120 on Cruise Ship En Route to Canada
item.description: A norovirus outbreak on the Princess Cruises-operated Ruby Princess cruise ship affected over 120 passengers and crew members, according to the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC). The ou...

item.title: Tanzania strengthens climate governance with 1.9bn/- international partnership
item.description: Tanzania has secured a 1.87 billion Tanzanian shilling (7 million Norwegian Kroner) partnership with the Royal Norwegian Embassy and UNDP to enhance climate governance. The initiative focuses on build...

item.title: Young Man Loses Job After TikTok Content Sparks Controversy
item.description: Daniel Seguton Chelimo, a 27-year-old from Baringo North, lost his ranching job after his employer discovered a viral TikTok video he shared. The video warned women about a scam where men tricked them...

item.title: FIFA Reschedules England-Mexico World Cup Match to 1am, Impacting Pubs and Schools
item.description: FIFA has confirmed that England's World Cup match against Mexico will proceed as originally planned at 01:00 BST on Monday, despite initial reports of a potential six-hour time shift. The decision fol...

item.title: Pope Leo Advocates for Migrant Protection in Lampedusa Amid Mediterranean Crisis
item.description: Pope Leo XIV emphasized the dignity of migrants during his visit to Lampedusa, an Italian island serving as a critical gateway to Europe. The Mediterranean, while symbolizing hope for many seeking bet...

item.title: Impeachment Trial of Vice President Sara Duterte Begins on July 6
item.description: The impeachment trial of Vice President Sara Duterte, the first Philippine public official to face impeachment twice by the House of Representatives, is set to begin on July 6. Four articles of impeac...

item.title: New Zealand Secures Narrow Victory Over France in Rugby Clash
item.description: In a thrilling rugby encounter, New Zealand's Dave Rennie-led team secured a dramatic 34-32 win against an understrength France squad in the first Nations Championship Test. The match, played at Chris...

item.title: Bena Sareen on Book Design Evolution and AI's Role
item.description: The article features Bena Sareen, a senior book designer in India, discussing her 26-year career in publishing. She emphasizes the importance of simplicity and minimalism in book cover design, especia...

item.title: LANXESS Launches Local Production of Fire-Resistant Hydraulic Fluids in India
item.description: LANXESS, a German specialty chemicals company, announced plans to commence local production of fire-resistant hydraulic fluids (FRHF) at its Jhagadia site in India. This move is part of its global gro...

item.title: Trump Announces Final July 4 Announcement
item.description: The article discusses a potential final announcement by Donald Trump on July 4, 2026, amid ongoing investigations and political discourse. It references various opinion pieces and analyses surrounding...

item.title: England aims to overcome Mexico at Azteca with Harry Kane's leadership
item.description: England's Morgan Rogers has emphasized the critical role of Harry Kane in their upcoming World Cup clash against Mexico at the Estadio Azteca. The match, a round-of-16 tie, is set to be a high-stakes ...

item.title: Portugal stays in Toronto following historic World Cup victory over Croatia
item.description: Portugal has extended its stay in Toronto after securing a dramatic 3-2 victory over Croatia in a thrilling World Cup match. The win, which saw Ronaldo's family dine at Canadian Portuguese restaurants...

item.title: Kenyan Gospel Artist Justina Syokau Shares Weight Loss Journey and Transformation
item.description: Kenyan gospel artist Justina Syokau has shared her journey of losing over 15 kilograms in two months, showcasing her transformation to her fans. In a video posted on her Facebook page, she appeared in...

item.title: PM Shehbaz urges Turkish firms to boost investments in Pakistan's key sectors
item.description: Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif, during his official visit to Turkey, emphasized Pakistan's commitment to strengthening economic ties with Turkey and attracting greater Turkish investment. The PM met wi...

item.title: Australian Superannuation Gains Boosted by Global Markets, Falling Petrol Prices, and ETF Growth
item.description: The article highlights key financial developments in Australia, including stronger overseas market returns boosting superannuation funds despite a modest 2.77% gain in the Australian sharemarket. Glob...

item.title: 2026 Browser Alternatives and AI Integration in Web Browsers
item.description: The article explores the evolving landscape of web browsers in 2026, emphasizing a shift from traditional search-focused competition to AI-driven functionalities. While Chrome and Safari remain domina...

item.title: New Africa Healthcare Hub Aims to Address Funding and Governance Challenges
item.description: A new center of excellence has been established in Africa to tackle persistent challenges in healthcare systems across the continent. The initiative focuses on improving health sector governance, fina...

item.title: UNHCR Warns of Escalating Crisis in Lake Chad Basin
item.description: The United Nations High Commissioner for Refugees (UNHCR) has raised alarms about the worsening security situation in the Lake Chad Basin, which spans parts of Cameroon, Chad, Niger, and Nigeria. Over...

item.title: MP College Cancels Exams After Papers Stolen, Police Investigate
item.description: The Rajiv Gandhi Proudyogiki Vishwavidyalaya (RGPV), a Madhya Pradesh government engineering university, canceled its fourth-semester computer exams for the school of biotechnology after examination p...
